executive_summary.root_cause: Wrong container element selector in getCanvasOffset() - uses .designer-editor (grandparent with 50px padding) instead of .designer-canvas-container (direct parent with 0px padding)
executive_summary.impact: Viewport-dependent coordinate calculation errors due to responsive CSS padding

dom_hierarchy_analysis.actual_structure: HTML structure from widget.php (lines 44-55):

padding_comparison.offset_calculation_error: Using .designer-editor includes 50px padding on desktop, 0px on mobile

bug_proof.offset_calculation_error: Uses grandparent padding instead of parent padding

desktop_scenario.result: Adds 50px offset to all image positions

mobile_scenario.result: Adds 0px offset to all image positions

bug_proof.viewport_dependency_risk: HIGH - Coordinates change based on screen width
bug_proof.data_corruption_risk: HIGH - Same design renders differently on desktop vs mobile

responsive_behavior_explanation.26_1px_origin: The 26.1px offset likely comes from a viewport width between breakpoints or partial zoom levels where the 50px padding is interpolated or browser applies sub-pixel rendering. This could also be from browser DevTools responsive mode or iframe scaling.

breakpoint_interaction.transition_zone: Exactly at 950px or browser zoom levels
breakpoint_interaction.26_1px_explanation: Likely caused by: (1) Browser zoom != 100%, (2) DevTools responsive mode with fractional scaling, (3) Viewport width near 950px breakpoint, (4) Sub-pixel rendering rounding

viewport_dependency_risk.issue: Offset calculation depends on CSS media query state
viewport_dependency_risk.consequence: Image positions stored in database include viewport-specific offsets
viewport_dependency_risk.data_integrity_issue: Design saved on desktop will render incorrectly on mobile and vice versa

correct_implementation_exists.note: This line correctly uses .parentNode which would be .designer-canvas-container

why_this_fixes_bug: 1. Uses direct parent (.designer-canvas-container) instead of grandparent (.designer-editor)
2. .designer-canvas-container has 0px padding on ALL viewports (desktop & mobile)
3. Eliminates responsive CSS dependency - padding is always 0px
4. Makes offset calculation viewport-independent and consistent
5. Fabric.js coordinates are already canvas-relative, so offset should always be 0,0

validation_proof.coordinate_consistency: Same coordinates regardless of viewport width, zoom, or device

additional_benefits: Faster execution (parentNode is direct property access vs DOM traversal)
More robust (doesn't depend on CSS class names)
Aligns with existing code at line 1399 which already uses this.canvas.parentNode
Fixes root cause: Canvas offset should be 0,0 relative to its direct parent

evidence_summary.padding_desktop_confirmed: ✓ CSS line 107-108 shows .designer-editor has 50px left/right padding on desktop
evidence_summary.padding_mobile_confirmed: ✓ CSS line 690-692 shows .designer-editor has 0px padding on mobile (<950px)
evidence_summary.container_zero_padding_confirmed: ✓ CSS lines 115-121 and 695-698 show .designer-canvas-container has NO padding rules (0px default)

regression_risk_analysis.breaking_change_risk: NONE - Fix removes incorrect offset addition
regression_risk_analysis.backward_compatibility: IMPROVES - Existing saved designs will render more correctly
testing_required: Desktop viewport (>950px) - verify images position correctly
Mobile viewport (<950px) - verify images position correctly
Existing saved designs - verify they load correctly

next_agent_instructions:
Agent 4: Mathematically explain the 26.1px vs 50px discrepancy. Investigate:
1. Why 26.1px instead of exactly 50px?
2. Is there browser zoom, iframe scaling, or DevTools responsive mode involved?
3. Are there other offset calculations in the codebase adding to the total?
4. Verify that after fix, offset should be exactly 0,0 in all scenarios
5. Check if legacy data correction logic is also contributing to the offset

action_items.action: Update source file (pre-bundle) with same fix
action_items.file: Find webpack source for getCanvasOffset() method

action_items.action: Test on desktop and mobile viewports
action_items.expected_outcome: Consistent image positioning regardless of viewport

action_items.action: Verify existing saved designs still load correctly
action_items.expected_outcome: No data corruption or rendering issues
